input.command: git merge-base --is-ancestor c7135580 HEAD && echo "yes ancestor" || echo "no"

input.content:
Bead ddx-51ac997b: no changes needed — all acceptance criteria already
satisfied by code already merged into HEAD.

Status of each AC against current HEAD (97b75512):

1. "Ladder type + Next() method in cli/internal/agent/escalation/"
   PRESENT: cli/internal/agent/escalation/ladder.go defines:
     - type Ladder
     - func NewLadder([]agentlib.ModelInfo) *Ladder
     - func (l *Ladder) Next(actualPower int) (int, error)
     - func (l *Ladder) Tiers() []int
   No vendor names appear; ladder reasons over Power tiers only.

2. "Unit tests cover all transitions"
   PRESENT: cli/internal/agent/escalation/ladder_test.go covers:
     - Tiers distinct/ascending and ignoring unrated (Power<=0) models
     - Next stepping through all-viable tiers, including strictly-greater
     - Exhaustion (ErrLadderExhausted) on top tier and empty/nil ladders
     - Skip-tier returning *NoViableProviderError, then bumping past it
     - All-non-viable catalog exhausting via repeated skips
     - Partial availability (Available xor AutoRoutable) counted non-viable

3. "Skip-tier returns typed NoViableProviderError"
   PRESENT: ladder.go declares `type NoViableProviderError struct{ Floor int }`
   with Error() method; Next returns &NoViableProviderError{Floor: tier}
   when the next tier above actualPower has no viable model.
   TestLadder_Next_SkipTierReturnsNoViableProviderError uses errors.As to
   confirm the typed error and Floor value.

4. "Tests assert ladder uses RoutingActual.Power as input for next-floor
   computation"
   PRESENT: TestLadder_Next_UsesRoutingActualPower constructs a stand-in
   RoutingActual record, passes prev.Power directly to Next, asserts the
   returned floor (90 from prev.Power=70), and additionally swaps
   Provider/Model/Harness to confirm Power is the sole signal.

Verification:
  $ cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/escalation/
  ok  	github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent/escalation	0.002s

Origin of the merged code: commit
  c7135580  feat(agent/escalation): MinPower-only ladder.Next with
            skip-tier NoViableProviderError [ddx-51ac997b]
which is an ancestor of current HEAD. The prior REVIEW:BLOCK note on this
bead was based on a different attempt's diff that contained only an
execution result.json artifact; that situation no longer applies — the
code, tests, and typed error are present in HEAD and tests pass.

No new commit produced because the bead's deliverable already exists in
the base revision (97b75512). The only working-tree delta is an unrelated
deletion of .agents/skills/sample-skill which is out of scope for this
bead and intentionally not committed here.

data.text:
All four ACs are already satisfied by code present in HEAD (commit `c7135580`, an ancestor of the base rev `97b75512`):

- `cli/internal/agent/escalation/ladder.go` — `Ladder` + `Next()`, no vendor names
- `cli/internal/agent/escalation/ladder_test.go` — covers all transitions, skip-tier, exhaustion, and `RoutingActual.Power` usage
- `NoViableProviderError` typed error with `Floor` field
- `go test ./internal/agent/escalation/` → ok

The prior REVIEW:BLOCK note referenced a different attempt whose diff contained only `result.json`; that no longer applies. Wrote `.ddx/executions/20260502T195552-15ac762e/no_changes_rationale.txt` documenting AC-by-AC evidence in HEAD. No commit produced.
